summary: Tested the new free-tier pricing model for MyBoard (Node.js + vanilla JS SPA). Backend: 12/13 pytest tests passing (1 skipped because driving the AI counter to 10 requires a configured anthropic key — verified via code review instead). Frontend: free-tier signup correctly lands on /home (no paywall auto-redirect), paywall view renders the new copy with €0.85 / €5.95 prices and the 'You're on the free plan · 0/10 AI generations used this month' note, the upgrade-close-btn returns the user to /home, aiErrorToast handles code='free_limit_reached' (shows the correct toast text and surfaces the paywall after a short delay), and the account menu shows 'Free plan' + '0 / 10 AI generations used' for the free user. One MAJOR UX issue found: account-upgrade-btn is hidden for free users in dev because the conditional requires billing.enabled=true.

minor.issue: Because anthropic is unconfigured in dev, /api/ai returns 503 BEFORE freeGenRecord() runs (server/index.js:798 vs :845). Counter can never be driven up via the API in dev, so the live 402 free_limit_reached path is untestable end-to-end without either (a) configuring anthropic or (b) a temporary admin endpoint that bumps ai_gen_count. Verified by code review (server/index.js:786-795) that the 402 path is correct and returns { code:'free_limit_reached', used, limit, resetAt }.

ui_bugs.issue: MAJOR: data-testid='account-upgrade-btn' is HIDDEN for free users in this dev environment because the conditional is `const showUpgrade = billing.enabled && !isPaidTier && !isGrandfathered` (homeView.js:517). When Stripe is unconfigured (billing.enabled=false), free users see only 'Free plan / 0 / 10 AI generations used / Sign out' — no Upgrade CTA in the menu. The review explicitly lists account-upgrade-btn as a required testid for free users. Should drop the billing.enabled gate (or keep the button visible and have it open the upgrade view, which itself already handles the unconfigured case).

When openUpgrade() is called before model.billing is populated (e.g. immediately after a fresh registration via API helper before refreshBilling resolves), the price card renders '€NaN for the first 30 days / then €NaN / month'. Cause: lines 26-35 fall back to `prices = { NOK:{}, EUR:{} }`, so p.setupAmount and p.monthlyAmount are undefined and Number(undefined)=NaN. Should either (a) have openUpgrade() await refreshBilling() before changePage('paywall'), or (b) defend in _fmtPrice when amount is missing.

issues: paywall-switch-currency link is not rendered in dev because the conditional `otherAvailable` requires Stripe to have configured price IDs for the alternate currency. In dev neither currency is available, so the testid never appears. Will work in prod once Stripe is configured — not a bug, just call-out for the testing checklist.

action_items: FIX (high): In public/views/homeView.js line 517 — drop the `billing.enabled &&` guard on showUpgrade so free users always see the Upgrade CTA in the account menu (or replace it with `!isPaidTier && !isGrandfathered` only). The upgrade view itself already gracefully handles the unconfigured-Stripe case via the 503 on /api/billing/checkout, so showing the button is safe.

FIX (medium): Make openUpgrade() in public/controller.js await refreshBilling() before changePage('paywall'), OR guard _fmtPrice() in paywallView.js to render '—' instead of 'NaN' when amount is undefined. Repro: log out, open the upgrade view via openUpgrade() before billing is fetched → price card shows €NaN.
OPTIONAL: Add a brief defensive 'Loading…' price card in paywallView when billing.prices is empty, to avoid the NaN flash on slow networks.
critical_code_review_comments: server/index.js:798 vs :845 ordering — the anthropic 503 returns BEFORE freeGenRecord() runs, so in dev environments without anthropic the counter never increments. That's intended (no generation = no charge), but it does mean the 402 free_limit_reached code path is untestable without either a configured Anthropic key or a test-only DB-bump endpoint. Consider adding a dev-only POST /api/_test/set-ai-count guarded by NODE_ENV !== 'production' to make E2E testing of the limit reached state easier in CI.

public/views/paywallView.js:35 — `const p = prices[safeCur] || {};` is the right defensive fallback at the dict level, but p.setupAmount / p.monthlyAmount can still be undefined when billing is empty. _fmtPrice should guard: `if (!Number.isFinite(n)) return '—';`.

public/views/homeView.js:517 — coupling the Upgrade CTA visibility to billing.enabled is too aggressive; it hides the only path-to-paywall from the account menu in any environment where Stripe isn't yet wired (staging, demos, local dev). Decouple the CTA visibility from billing.enabled — the button can still open the (non-blocking) upgrade view even when checkout itself would 503.
server/index.js has reached 867 lines and mixes auth, billing, AI, and data routes in one file. >700-line threshold breached — consider splitting into server/routes/{auth,billing,ai,data}.js modules.

success_rate.backend: 100% (12/12 executed tests; 1 skipped due to env limitation)
success_rate.frontend: 85% (10/12 spec items verified, account-upgrade-btn hidden in dev, paywall-switch-currency untestable in dev)

test_credentials: pricetest1 / testpw (verified working). /app/memory/test_credentials.md lists testuser1/pass1234 but that user no longer exists in the current DB (login returns 401) — main agent may want to refresh that doc.
seed_data_creation: Backend tests register fresh users with TEST_pt_<timestamp> prefix on each run (free tier, never used trial). They are not cleaned up — sql.js DB will accumulate them over time. Not critical for now.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Main blocker is the account-upgrade-btn visibility regression (homeView.js:517). Once the main agent removes the `billing.enabled &&` guard on showUpgrade, re-run the existing pytest suite (it should still 100% pass — the change is frontend-only) and re-open the account menu via the username pill (button.pill) and confirm `[data-testid="account-upgrade-btn"]` is now present. The aiErrorToast → paywall navigation already works correctly. paywall-switch-currency cannot be tested in dev — defer to staging once Stripe price IDs are wired. To test free_limit_reached live, either configure ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in /app/backend/.env or add a dev-only setAiCount endpoint.
rca of the issue: account-upgrade-btn missing: the conditional `const showUpgrade = billing.enabled && !isPaidTier && !isGrandfathered` at public/views/homeView.js:517 short-circuits to false whenever billing.enabled is false. billing.enabled comes from billingConfigured() on the server (server/index.js:45-50) which returns false unless at least one fully wired Stripe currency exists. In the dev container ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and STRIPE_*_TEST env vars are intentionally unset, so the testid disappears. The fix is to remove the billing.enabled prerequisite — the upgrade view is now non-blocking and the upgrade button can navigate to it whether or not Stripe checkout is wired.
